relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul reaches Ephesus and finds disciples still at an earlier threshold.
The question is whether holy Spirit has been received.
Their baptism is John’s turning-water.
John’s baptism points forward into trust in Jesus.
They are baptized into the Lord Jesus’ name.
Hands are laid, holy Spirit comes, tongues and prophecy flow.
About twelve are gathered into this new fullness.
Paul speaks boldly about the Divine reign.
When the Way is maligned, teaching moves to Tyrannus’ school.
Two years carry the word through Asia to Judeans and Greeks.
Extraordinary powers move through Paul’s hands.
Even contact-cloths become signs of healing and release.
Some try to use Jesus’ name as borrowed technique.
The sons of Sceva attempt this name without belonging to its life.
The harmful spirit recognizes Jesus and Paul but not the imitators.
Borrowed power collapses into exposure and wounds.
Fear falls and the name of Jesus is magnified.
Trusting ones bring hidden practices into confession.
Magic scrolls burn as old economies of power are renounced.
The word grows and prevails by the Lord’s power.
Paul sets in Spirit toward Jerusalem and eventually Rome.
Timothy and Erastus are sent ahead while Paul remains in Asia.
The Way provokes a disturbance.
Demetrius’ trade in Artemis shrines is threatened.
Economic prosperity rallies the craftsmen.
The handmade-gods critique destabilizes the trade.
Profit, temple honor, and civic identity fuse into panic.
Rage chants Artemis’ greatness.
Confusion fills the city and seizes Paul’s companions.
Paul wants to enter the crowd, but disciples restrain him.
Friendly officials urge him away from the theater.
The assembly is confused, many not knowing why they gathered.
Alexander is pushed forward for defense.
Recognition of his Judean identity intensifies the chant.
The city clerk appeals to civic certainty and order.
He calls for quiet and no recklessness.
The accused are not temple-robbers or goddess-blasphemers.
Legal courts are the place for charges.
Further matters belong to lawful assembly.
The riot itself risks accusation.
The assembly is dismissed.
